MUFG's stronger profile adds governance alignment; Fitch sees synergies in product development.

news.title:
GIC and MAS sold 266,269 Shriram Finance shares, reducing their stake to 4.27%.
The sale was executed in the open market on April 9, 2026, triggering disclosure under SEBI rules.

news.title:
Shriram Finance's long-term credit rating upgraded to AAA post MUFG's ₹39,618 cr investment.
MUFG's stake aids lower borrowing costs, profitability, and strengthens SFL's market leadership in NBFCs.

news.title:
Shriram Finance among top Nifty gainers as market ends six-week decline.
Midcap gainers include Adani Green, Ashok Leyland, Lodha, and Cholamandalam Investment.

news.title:
Nifty 50 gained 5.5% this week, its best since Feb 2021. Eight stocks saw weekly gains over 10%.
Shriram Finance (+14.5%), Adani Enterprises (+13.3%), Axis Bank (+12.8%), Tata Motors PV (+11.7%), Bajaj Auto (+11.1%), Bajaj Finance (+11.2%), Eicher Motors (+10.5%), and Trent (+10%) led the rally.
news.summary:
Nifty 50 gained 5.5% this week, its best since Feb 2021. Eight stocks saw weekly gains over 10%.
Shriram Finance (+14.5%), Adani Enterprises (+13.3%), Axis Bank (+12.8%), Tata Motors PV (+11.7%), Bajaj Auto (+11.1%), Bajaj Finance (+11.2%), Eicher Motors (+10.5%), and Trent (+10%) led the rally.

news.title:
Morgan Stanley maintains Overweight on Shriram Finance, sets target price at ₹1,325 after ICRA's AAA upgrade post MUFG deal.
Improved credit profile, narrowing funding costs, and ROA enhancement make it a top NBFC pick for Morgan Stanley.
news.summary:
Morgan Stanley maintains Overweight on Shriram Finance, sets target price at ₹1,325 after ICRA's AAA upgrade post MUFG deal.
Improved credit profile, narrowing funding costs, and ROA enhancement make it a top NBFC pick for Morgan Stanley.

news.title:
Nifty surged 3.78% to close near 23,997 after a ceasefire in West Asia boosted sentiments.
Shriram Finance and Adani Enterprises led gains; Nifty Auto and Realty rose over 6% each.
news.summary:
Nifty surged 3.78% to close near 23,997 after a ceasefire in West Asia boosted sentiments.

news.title:
MUFGBank acquired 4.71 Cr shares (20%) of Shriram Finance via preferential allotment.
Shares priced at ₹840.93 each; post-acquisition total equity capital is ₹4705.4 Cr.

news.title:
MUFG acquires 20% stake in Shriram Finance for ₹39,618 cr, marking India's largest cross-border financial deal.
Shriram Finance aims to scale operations post-investment, focusing on underserved markets and adopting global risk practices.
news.summary:
MUFG acquires 20% stake in Shriram Finance for ₹39,618 cr, marking India's largest cross-border financial deal.
Shriram Finance aims to scale operations post-investment, focusing on underserved markets and adopting global risk practices.

news.title:
Shriram Finance approves allotment of 47.1 Cr equity shares to MUFG Bank at Rs.840.93/share.
The issuance raises Rs.39,617.9 Cr, increasing paid-up equity capital to Rs.470.5 Cr.
news.summary:
Shriram Finance approves allotment of 47.1 Cr equity shares to MUFG Bank at Rs.840.93/share.
The issuance raises Rs.39,617.9 Cr, increasing paid-up equity capital to Rs.470.5 Cr.

It outpaced NBFC peers by 1.5%, signaling stock-specific strength.

news.title:
Motilal Oswal highlights NBFCs like Shriram Finance, PNB Housing Finance, L&T Finance, and Aditya Birla Capital as top picks.
Brokerage expects growth in net interest income, pre-provision operating profit, and PAT for NBFCs ahead of Q4 results.
news.summary:
Motilal Oswal highlights NBFCs like Shriram Finance, PNB Housing Finance, L&T Finance, and Aditya Birla Capital as top picks.
Brokerage expects growth in net interest income, pre-provision operating profit, and PAT for NBFCs ahead of Q4 results.

news.title:
Antique initiates 'Buy' on Shriram Finance, sees 28% upside with a FY28 target of ₹1,150, citing scalable growth and strong asset quality.
Shriram Finance's diversified portfolio, stable margins, and improved asset quality post-merger drive bullish outlook despite near-term risks.
news.summary:
Antique initiates 'Buy' on Shriram Finance, sees 28% upside with a FY28 target of ₹1,150, citing scalable growth and strong asset quality.
Shriram Finance's diversified portfolio, stable margins, and improved asset quality post-merger drive bullish outlook despite near-term risks.
